function.name: calculator
function.description:
Evaluate a mathematical expression safely.

Supports: +, -, *, /, //, %, **, abs, round, min, max, sqrt, log, log10,
sin, cos, tan. Constants: pi, e.

expression.description: A math expression to evaluate, e.g.   or  .

function.name: file_reader
function.description:
Read the contents of a file in the workspace.

Returns the file text or an error message if the file cannot be read.

path.description: Path to the file, relative to the workspace root.

max_size_kb.description: Maximum file size in KB to read. Defaults to 512.

function.name: local_file_search
function.description:
Search for files matching a pattern, or list directory contents.

Returns matching file paths (one per line) or an empty result message.

query.description: Filename pattern to search for (glob, e.g.   or  ). Omit to list all files.

directory.description: Subdirectory to search in, relative to workspace root. Defaults to root.

max_depth.description: Maximum recursion depth for search. Defaults to 3.

function.name: table_analyzer
function.description:
Analyze tabular data from a file or inline text.

Supports CSV and TSV. Call with action='summary' for an overview,
action='head:10' for first 10 rows, or action='columns' for column names.

source.description: Path to a CSV/TSV file (relative to workspace), or inline CSV/TSV text.

action.description: What to do:   (row count, columns, types),   (first N rows, N defaults to 5),   (list column names), or   (filter rows, e.g.  ).

max_rows.description: Maximum rows to process. Defaults to 1000.

function.name: format_converter
function.description:
Convert data between common formats.

Formats supported: json, csv, yaml, txt.
For CSV conversion, the data must be tabular (list of dicts, or
list-of-lists with a header row).

input_data.description: The data to convert (inline text, or a path relative to the workspace).

from_format.description: Source format: json, csv, yaml, or txt.

to_format.description: Target format: json, csv, yaml, or txt.

indent.description: Indentation for JSON/YAML output. Defaults to 2.
